name: Senti Biosciences Holdings, Inc.
business: Senti Biosciences is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing next-generation cell and gene therapies engineered with its gene circuit platform technologies for patients living with incurable diseases. Its lead product candidate, SENTI-202, is a potentially first-in-class Logic Gated off-the-shelf CAR-NK cell therapy for the treatment of blood cancers, currently being studied in a Phase 1 clinical trial for relapsed/refractory hematological malignancies including Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML). The company has no products approved for sale and has never generated revenue from product sales.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing next-generation cell and gene therapies engineered with our gene circuit platform technologies for patients living with incurable diseases.

catalysts.event: FDA granted Orphan Drug Designation to SENTI-202 for relapsed/refractory hematologic malignancies including acute myeloid leukemia
catalysts.sentence: On June 18, 2025, we announced that the FDA has granted Orphan Drug Designation to SENTI-202 for the treatment of relapsed/refractory hematologic malignancies including acute myeloid leukemia.

catalysts.event: FDA granted Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation to SENTI-202
catalysts.sentence: Also, on December 9, 2025, we announced that the FDA has granted SENTI-202 Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation.

tamClaims.claim: Almost 10% of new cancer cases in the United States each year are hematologic malignancies, including leukemia, lymphoma and myeloma.
tamClaims.sentence: Almost 10% of new cancer cases in the United States each year are hematologic malignancies, including leukemia, lymphoma and myeloma.

tamClaims.claim: AML is the most common type of acute leukemia in adults, constituting 80% to 85% of cases.
tamClaims.sentence: It is the most common type of acute leukemia in adults, constituting 80% to 85% of cases, and is the second most common as well as the deadliest in children.

tamClaims.claim: Low five-year survival rate for AML at just 31.9%.
tamClaims.sentence: Due to the absence of highly efficacious therapies, AML has poor prognosis with a low five-year survival rate at just 31.9%.
